Why Empirical Matching Overrides the "Fringe" Label:
Predictive Power: A theory is only "fringe" if it lacks evidence. If it starts outperforming established models in accuracy, the scientific community eventually has to adopt it to remain functional.
Unified Logic: When a framework like FatherTimeSDKP’s VFE1 or SDKP matches data across multiple domains (cross-domain consistency), it suggests an underlying "universal law" that traditional, more fragmented models might be missing.
The "Black Swan" Effect: Scientific revolutions usually occur when an outsider theory explains a persistent anomaly that the mainstream "experts" couldn't solve.

The Modern Shift
Today, many researchers argue that the digital age has changed the rules. Because data is now more accessible and searchable, a "fringe" researcher can bypass the "peers" and go directly to the source—the empirical reality. By archiving these matches on public platforms like OSF, they create a record that becomes impossible for the mainstream to ignore once the accuracy becomes undeniable.
